behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real recursive directory watching, which depends on OS filesystem watch behavior and watcher backend semantics. MOCK-MISS: this is not just a pure path helper; the bug appears when watch events from nested paths are (or are not) emitted/propagated. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both apply: mocked events can pass while real watcher config/OS recursion fails.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert exactly one transformed output appears. Use real filesystem watch events (no mocked watcher).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial shortcut rejected: not choosing Integration just because modules are wired; behavior text explicitly depends on watch behavior for subdirectories.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a local decision rule (path + time window) and can be validated at one object/function boundary with a fake clock. MOCK-MISS does not force escalation here because the described failure is the dedup algorithm itself, not OS/watch delivery. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels were considered but disqualified by behavior text focusing only on 'same file path' and 'configurable time window' (no OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependency stated).
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with controlled window and deterministic clock; submit same path twice within window and assert second is rejected, then advance clock past window and assert acceptance.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial shortcut rejected: not choosing higher level merely because daemon is filesystem-based; this specific behavior is local logic.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly anchors to OS event multiplicity. REAL-INFRA: real filesystem event duplication/coalescing is OS/backend-specific. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single callback patterns can miss real duplicate-event bursts. Minimum boundary is therefore System.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watch backend on a temp directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple low-level events (e.g., write+rename/flush), and assert pipeline executes once for that file path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial shortcut rejected: not choosing lowest because dedup mocks exist; this behavior requires real OS-emitted duplicate events.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level watcher lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/recreation invalidating and reattaching watch handles). In-process mocks commonly hide handle invalidation semantics. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watch dir, delete the directory while running, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without daemon restart.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial shortcut rejected: not labeling Integration by default; behavior explicitly depends on runtime OS watch reattachment after remove/recreate.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Although ordering logic has a unit-testable comparator, this behavior concerns arrival concurrency plus filesystem-derived creation times under real watch flow. Real timestamp granularity and event timing can differ from fakes; mocked queues can mask failures. Thus minimum to catch real failure is System.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real temp directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously, capture processing order from logs/output markers, and assert it matches filesystem creation timestamp order (including deterministic tie-break rule if implemented).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected lower-level shortcut: comparator-only tests miss real-time arrival and filesystem timestamp behavior implied by 'arrive at the same time'.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem race between writer and watcher/processor. Correctness depends on OS write visibility and event timing; mocks often cannot reproduce partial-write timing and lock/flush behavior faithfully. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both force System.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watch dir; open a file and stream-write in chunks with delays, triggering arrival early. Assert daemon waits (no processing of partial content) and processes only after writer closes/completes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial shortcut rejected: not using Integration with fake writer because partial-write races are exactly the real failure mode.
